title: lora_l2.c hwinfo_get_device_id returns ssize_t, compared with size_t
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:131, 138

## Finding
```c
ssize_t hwid_len;
hwid_len = hwinfo_get_device_id(hwid, sizeof(hwid));
if (hwid_len <= 0) { ... }
if ((size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid)) { ... }
```

The comparison `(size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid)` is safe here because the `<= 0` check above ensures `hwid_len` is positive before the cast. However, the cast is performed on a potentially negative value if hwinfo_get_device_id returns a negative error code that somehow passed the first check (would require a bug in the conditional).

## What goes wrong
In the current code, nothing goes wrong - the logic is correct. However, the pattern is fragile:
1. If someone refactors and changes the `<= 0` to `< 0`, negative zero (-0.0 converted) could slip through
2. The cast obscures the type mismatch that a compiler might otherwise warn about

## Recommendation
Make the bounds check explicit with the original type:
```c
if (hwid_len < 0 || (size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid)) {
    ...
}
```
Or use a local `size_t hwid_len_unsigned = (size_t)hwid_len;` after validation.
close_reason: Non-issue: The code already handles the signed/unsigned comparison correctly. Line 132 checks hwid_len <= 0 first (handles errors), then line 138 uses an explicit (size_t)hwid_len cast before comparing with sizeof(hwid). Line 146 also uses an explicit (size_t) cast. All signed/unsigned comparisons are properly handled with explicit casts.
